_note: Adoption seed for Battlemage. Tiers reflect the current verify.ps1 wiring; oracleSha256 values are pinned with `crucible approve` at landing time. Golden rules with no mechanical gate are declared T3 with a reason, which is the point of the first pass: it makes the prose-vs-enforced gap explicit.

gates.rule: GOLDEN_RULES §6 — no reward-hacked tests (skips, focused, copied impls, CI retries)
GOLDEN_RULES §8 — frontend↔Tauri command/type contract stays in sync
GOLDEN_RULES §13 — typed, indexed, linked knowledge docs
production module E2E flow matrix is covered
native feature E2E matrix is covered
change-to-crate impact selection is correct and fail-closed
durable docs cover the code they describe
gates.reason: advisory doc-quality signal; runs in qualification, not required per change

gates.rule: docs are not stale relative to the code they anchor
gates.reason: advisory; freshness heuristics are noisy, kept out of the required lane
advisory; broken-link report, non-blocking on the inner loop

gates.rule: changed lines have their mutants killed (the keystone un-gameable metric)
gates.reason: PROMOTION TARGET (Plank B): currently qualification-only with continue_on_failure; move to T1 tiered — blocking on money/checkout crates, advisory elsewhere
necessary-not-sufficient; coverage shows reachability, mutation shows behavior-checking — keep as milestone under the mutation keystone

gates.rule: GOLDEN_RULES §16 — squash before updating shared history
GOLDEN_RULES §1 — no drive-by refactors or unrelated worktree changes
gates.reason: human-judgement rule; no mechanical checker distinguishes a drive-by from an in-scope change. Partially observed by Summoner scope claims for delegated work.

gates.rule: GOLDEN_RULES §3 — okayserver is the brain, battlemage the hands
gates.reason: architectural boundary enforced by review, not a checker; a false positive would block legitimate client-side work
policy statement, not a code invariant

gates.rule: GOLDEN_RULES §9 — respect crate dependency directions; keep common light
gates.reason: no dependency-direction structural test yet. Candidate to promote to T1 with a cargo-metadata boundary checker (the OpenAI-harness pattern).

gates.rule: GOLDEN_RULES §15 — effort/duration are not scoping criteria
gates.reason: process rule about planning language, not mechanically checkable
